title: Telegram bot: persistent peer-switch keyboard with fresh data + retry-on-failure
description: Replace the sticky-/select + inline-button routing model with a persistent Telegram ReplyKeyboardMarkup that's always visible below the compose bar. One tap to switch peers, current target marked with ✦, recents filtered against live daemon /peers data, live placeholder showing who you're talking to. On send failure, refresh the keyboard with fresh peer data and stash a pending-retry intent: the next peer-tap replays the failed message to that peer (cancellable by typing new text). Inline buttons used only for the '…' full-peer expansion. Rationale: real-usage observations (notif-978a6725) — @-tag-in-prose and one-word replies to recent notifiers dominate; sticky /select goes stale; inline buttons add visual noise and scrollback hell. See design convo in session repowire-claude-code 2026-04-23.
design: Persistent ReplyKeyboardMarkup rebuilt on every outgoing bot message. Markers: ✦ current, 💬 recent, … open full picker. Placeholder shows 'msg @target…' or 'No active peer'. Recents = in-memory deque(maxlen=10), filtered against fresh /peers on each rebuild. Failure path: refetch /peers, rebuild keyboard, store pending_retry=(text, expires_at, ~60s TTL); on next peer-switch tap within window, resend original text to new peer; on typed message, clear retry. /keyboard off escape hatch.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Persistent keyboard appears on every bot message; 2. Tap ✦/💬 peer switches target with one tap, no typing; 3. Placeholder reflects current target live; 4. On /notify failure, keyboard refreshes + pending-retry set; 5. Peer-tap during retry window resends original message; 6. Typing new text cancels retry; 7. /keyboard off removes it; 8. Unit tests for keyboard builder, prefix parser, recents dedup+online-filter, retry TTL.
notes: Implemented on feat/telegram-persistent-keyboard. 25 new unit tests, 316 total pass, ruff+ty clean. Not pushed — awaiting Prass review.

title: Adopt beads for issue tracking
description: Replace TodoWrite/markdown TODO lists with beads (bd) for all task tracking in this repo. Includes: initialize .beads/ workspace, commit config.yaml + hooks + metadata, update CLAUDE.md with beads workflow section, add session-close protocol requiring git push before calling work complete.
close_reason: Done in working tree: .beads/ initialized with config+hooks+metadata, CLAUDE.md updated with beads section and session-close protocol. Pending commit.

title: daemon: /notify should distinguish delivered vs queued vs unknown peer
description: Today /notify returns 200 for both 'delivered to online peer' and 'queued for offline peer', and 404 only when the peer name doesn't resolve at all. Clients (Telegram bot, dashboard) can't tell the difference between a real delivery and a queued-for-later, so they can't surface accurate status (e.g. ⏳ vs 👍). Proposed: richer response shape like {delivered: bool, queued: bool, status: 'online'|'offline'|'unknown'}. Surfaced while designing the Telegram persistent keyboard — bot has to guess offline state from its own cached /peers list instead of getting an authoritative signal from the notify response.
acceptance_criteria: 1. /notify response distinguishes delivered, queued, and unknown-peer cases; 2. Existing 200/404 contract preserved or versioned; 3. Telegram bot and dashboard can consume the new shape without guessing.

title: Silence curl output in tmux rename hooks
description: The after-rename-session/window tmux hooks shell out to curl, which prints the JSON response body ({"ok":true}) to stdout. tmux's run-shell captures that and flashes it in the status bar on every rename — noisy. Fix: redirect curl stdout to /dev/null in repowire/hooks/tmux_rename_hook.sh and the inline curl commands in repowire/hooks/tmux_lifecycle.py.
